message:
security(checkpoint): harden storage against corrupt/malformed on-disk data with fail-closed validation

Replace silent failures and tolerant-but-unsafe code paths with strict
validation and informative errors:

**Storage safety (storage.go):**
Delete reimplemented via deleteCheckpointPath which validates session
name, checkpoint ID, and rejects symlinked or non-directory session
paths before os.RemoveAll. selectionCandidateEntry filters by valid
checkpoint ID format and excludes incremental namespace directory.
New HasCheckpointCandidates exposes whether checkpoint-shaped entries
exist even if none load cleanly.

**Auto-checkpoint rotation/retrieval (auto.go):**
rotateAutoCheckpoints validates retained checkpoints before deleting
older ones (blocks deletion on invalid retained entries).
autoCheckpointCandidates extracted as reusable helper using ID-based
fast-path detection. GetLastAutoCheckpoint propagates errors from
corrupt newest auto-checkpoints. BackgroundWorker.Start goroutine
wrapper ensures context cancel func always called.

**Ref parsing (capture.go):**
ParseCheckpointRef switches from Exists (directory-only) to
HasCheckpointPath (validates not a symlink or plain file), so broken
paths fail closed instead of silently falling through.

**CLI surface (cli/checkpoint.go):**
List commands surface sessions with only-invalid checkpoints (previously
silently skipped), emit invalid_checkpoints_present in JSON output, and
propagate errors instead of swallowing them.

**Tests:** Extensive new coverage for invalid retained checkpoints,
symlink/non-directory ID paths, incremental namespace filtering, and
CLI invalid-session reporting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
